title: Checkout Conversion Drop
problem_statement: Weekly orders have dropped by 18% over the past month. Competitors are maintaining steady volume. Your leadership team needs a data-backed explanation and recovery plan.
root_cause_detail: PayStream v3 payment gateway migration on Jan 10 introduced a latency regression in the iOS payment path, causing timeouts and failures that disproportionately affect iOS and Apple Pay users.

analyst.description: Business metrics, funnels, and segment analysis

ux_researcher.description: User feedback, support tickets, usability research, UX change history

engineering_lead.description: Deployment history, service health metrics, system architecture, error patterns

expected_key_findings: Orders declining since Jan 10 while DAU remains stable — users visit but can't complete orders
Payment step has highest funnel drop-off (93% → ~55% post Jan 10)
iOS hit 6x worse than Android (-30% vs -5% order change)
Apple Pay worst affected payment method (~45% success rate)
PayStream v3 migration deployed Jan 10 correlates exactly with decline start
Payment service p99 latency spiked from 450ms to 4,500ms (10x)
Error codes shifted to GATEWAY_TIMEOUT and PAYMENT_PROVIDER_ERROR
Jan 14 hotfix (timeout increase) briefly helped then regressed
iOS users explicitly reporting payment hangs and switching to competitors
Usability study shows task completion dropped from 92% to 54%

red_herrings.signal: Holiday promo Dec 26-31 caused DAU spike
red_herrings.why_misleading: Candidate might think 'traffic dropped after promo ended' but the real decline starts Jan 10, not Jan 1
Resolved by Jan 9, doesn't correlate with Jan 10+ decline — it was an index rebuild
Unrelated email provider issue, tests if candidate stays focused on payment thread
Partly holiday calendar differences, not solely payment issues — tests multi-factor thinking

red_herrings.signal: UX changelog shows 'payment confirmation animation' change on Jan 10
red_herrings.why_misleading: A UI animation change, not the backend payment migration — tests if candidate distinguishes UI from infra

red_herrings.signal: Promo code WINTER25 bug in support tickets
red_herrings.why_misleading: Normal operational issue unrelated to the order decline

levels.excellent: Identifies PayStream v3 migration as root cause, explains iOS-specific latency regression, links deployment date to metric decline
levels.good: Identifies payment service issue and iOS correlation but may not pinpoint the exact deployment
levels.adequate: Identifies payment step as problematic but doesn't isolate the technical trigger
levels.poor: Blames wrong factor (promo ending, UI changes, general traffic decline)

levels.excellent: Systematically queries all 3 agents, cross-references findings, avoids red herrings, forms and refines hypotheses
levels.good: Queries multiple agents, makes connections between data sources
levels.adequate: Uses agents but doesn't effectively cross-reference or gets distracted by red herrings
levels.poor: Only queries one agent, doesn't explore multiple data dimensions

levels.excellent: Tiered action plan (immediate rollback + short-term fixes + long-term prevention), user recovery plan, monitoring improvements, data-backed impact quantification
levels.good: Structured actions including rollback and follow-up improvements
levels.adequate: Proposes rollback but lacks depth on prevention or recovery

levels.excellent: Frames for leadership (revenue impact, competitive risk, timeline), clear stakeholder narrative
levels.good: Clear summary with supporting data points
levels.adequate: Identifies issue but explanation is disorganized
levels.poor: Unclear communication, dumps raw data without synthesis
